She has deep expertise in Kubernetes, GitOps, DevOps, and DevSecOps, helping organizations accelerate and scale cloud-native adoption. Dolis has built and managed mission-critical systems and successfully led multiple environments through rigorous security certifications, including SOC 2 and ISO 27001. Known for translating complex technical challenges into tangible business outcomes, she partners closely with enterprise teams, offering architecture guidance, automation strategies, and operational best practices. When she’s not exploring the world of technology, she enjoys hiking, chasing waterfalls, relaxing on the beach, or dancing. She holds a diploma in Kathak and loves moving to the rhythm of the music

person.name: Erdi Erkan
person.bio: As an engineer who is continuously improving in the fields of cloud technologies and DevOps, I work on modern infrastructure management, automation, and CI/CD processes. I aim to design scalable, secure, and efficient systems. With my passion for technology, I strive to create innovative solutions and keep advancing within the cloud ecosystem.

person.name: Eric Solis-Arellano
person.bio: Hi, I'm Eric — a Cloud & Infrastructure Architect with deep experience designing and operating secure, scalable, multi-cloud environments across AWS, Azure, and Kubernetes-based platforms. I specialize in modern cloud architectures and microservices, helping organizations move from legacy or fragmented systems into resilient, observable, and secure cloud-native platforms. My background blends infrastructure engineering, cloud security, and platform architecture, allowing me to design solutions that are not only scalable-but defensible, cost-aware, and production-ready. Outside of work, you'll usually find me indoor rock climbing or fueling up with a meticulously brewed cup of coffee.

person.name: Thiago Finardi
person.bio: Thiago is a Principal Platform Engineer at Itau Unibanco, focused on observability, performance, and resilience in large-scale distributed systems. He collaborated on the CNCF Certified Cloud Native Platform Engineering Associate (CNPA) certification and supports Cloud Native events in Florianopolis, Sao Paulo, and online. Acting as a connector among multiple Community Groups across Brazil, he strengthens collaboration and knowledge sharing around Kubernetes, Chaos Engineering, and Open Observability.

person.name: Winson Sou
person.bio: Advisory Solutions Architect at Nutanix, covering Cloud Native & AI. Cloud Native enthusiast. As a Kubestronaut, I aim to empower organizations to harness the full potential of Kubernetes for scalable, intelligent, and future-ready platforms.

person.name: Ei Ei Mon
person.bio: I’m a Software Engineer specializing in web application development. I began exploring cloud-native technologies in 2024 and have since been actively expanding my knowledge within the CNCF ecosystem. My interests include container orchestration, cloud-native security, and designing scalable, resilient architectures. I’m passionate about continuously strengthening my expertise and contributing to the cloud-native community in the future.

person.name: Feynman Zhou
person.bio: Feynman is a product manager for Microsoft Azure. He is also a maintainer for CNCF ORAS and Notary Project. He is a big fan of open source and has been contributing to multiple CNCF projects over five years. He is a speaker of KubeCon and enjoy technology advocacy. He also hosted several events, such as Kubernetes Community Days China, CNCF Webinar, and cloud native meetups.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I’m super excited to be part of the Cloud Native Ambassadors family. I would like to work with other ambassador peers to learn and share cloud-native technologies.

person.name: Andy Peng
person.bio: PNW local. Seasoned software product builder and technologist based in Seattle (WA). Expertise in large-scale distributed systems and cloud computing: AI, ML + Data, containers & virtualization, serverless compute, open-source. Diverse experience encompassing both people management and technical leadership, including cross-company collaborations. Community Lead of Cloud Native Seattle. Technical advisory board. Guest lecturer of University of Washington. Photographer, Music lover, have a taylor made in walnut, and I love Icewine. Homepage: https://pengandy.com/Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am thrilled to be joining the CNCF Ambassador program. As someone who is deeply passionate about building and promoting fundamental technologies, particularly in the realm of Cloud Native solutions, I feel grateful for the opportunity to deepen my connections with the community and share my knowledge and experience with open-source users around the globe. By being part of this program, I hope to make a broader impact and bring even more value to Linux Foundation and CNCF. Additionally, I am eager to learn more about the needs of users and help them understand how they can effectively use cloud native and container technology to solve their problems.

person.name: Louise Champ
person.bio: I'm a DevOps engineer with nearly 10 years of experience managing production Kubernetes clusters and working with CNCF projects. My work spans architecting and building platforms from scratch through to operational concerns including security, observability, GitOps workflows and pipelines, multi-cluster platform management, developer experience, and cluster lifecycle management, whilst staying engaged with the fast-evolving best practices across the ecosystem. I regularly write technical content exploring practical and timely implementation challenges, and enjoy making complex Kubernetes concepts more accessible.

person.name: Roman Willi
person.bio: In my current role as a Cloud Software Engineer, I  am working on cloud native operators in  the security field.  My academic background includes a Master of Science (M.Sc.) in Computer Science from ETH Zurich, with focus in Information Systems, Software Engineering, and Network Security.

person.name: Alex Parra
person.bio: As a Platform and DevOps Engineer, I'm passionate about cloud-native technologies and the craft of building reliable, scalable platforms. I specialise in Kubernetes, GitOps, and AWS, with strong expertise in Istio, ArgoCD, and Terraform. I enjoy creating automation and internal developer platforms that improve developer experience and operational excellence. Beyond engineering, I thrive by mentoring communities such as Cloud Native Querétaro and contributing as an AWS Community Builder. My top favourite technologies are Kubernetes, Istio, ArgoCD, Argo Rollouts, and I love blending technical depth with community impact.

person.name: Audra Montenegro
person.bio: Over the past eleven years, Audra has evolved from organizing global events, to empowering others to do the same, to becoming a speaker herself. For seven of those years, she led programming for both major tech conferences and intimate summits at O’Reilly Media.Audra’s passion for community building and her expertise in event management have made her a sought-after speaker at conferences such as KubeCon + CloudNativeCon, DevOpsDays, and DevOps World. She has also been a featured speaker at the Open Source Summit, where she shared her insights on the intersection of open source and community engagement.Since 2022, Audra has served as a Community and Outreach Manager at the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF), where she drives global initiatives like Kubernetes Community Days (KCDs), Cloud Native Community Groups (CNCGs), the ChatLoopBackOff (CLBO) livestream series, and CNCF’s member video and streaming programs.Audra is fueled by a commitment to elevate emerging voices and deepen community engagement. Her passion lies in connecting industry leaders, spotlighting technical innovators, and creating platforms where ideas spark, grow, and resonate globally.

person.name: Bálint Kaszás
person.bio: I started as an intern at NTT Data (then still Dimension Data), where I dealt with network and data center virtualization. In addition, I wrote small applications in python and script languages, which helped clients perform various integrations efficiently. I really like to discover and learn new things, which is why I came to Kubernetes, which contains an inexhaustible number of integrations and components.

person.name: Egy Ardian
person.bio: I’m a DevOps and Cloud Engineer with experience in automation, infrastructure management, and FinOps. My work spans Kubernetes operations, CI/CD engineering, and cloud cost optimization, where I’ve helped improve reliability, streamline deployments, and reduce costs. I enjoy research and innovation, mentoring teams, and leading workshops to share knowledge. Outside of work, I’m a normal guy who enjoys learning new technologies and experimenting with creative cloud-native solutions. also, I Love Cats.

person.name: Gichul Hong
person.bio: Started career in the financial/communications field with Java.  Have been working on bigdata and on premise Kubernetes for several years. Love simple and intuitive systems.

person.name: Radu Dobrinescu
person.bio: Senior Cloud Solutions Architect specializing in cloud-native architectures and Kubernetes orchestration. Focusing on designing scalable solutions using Amazon EKS and implementing AI/ML training and inference on Kubernetes. Actively involved in the AWS Bucharest User Group Meetup and passionate about helping organizations modernize through cloud-native technologies.

person.name: Ricardo Rocha
person.bio: Ricardo leads the Platform Infrastructure team at CERN with a strong focus on cloud native deployments and machine learning. He has led for several years the internal effort to transition services and workloads to use cloud native technologies, as well as dissemination and training efforts. Ricardo got CERN to join the CNCF and is a lead of the CNCF Research User Group. Prior to this work Ricardo helped develop the grid computing infrastructure serving the Large Hadron Collider (LHC).

person.name: Ryosuke Kato
person.bio: I am a Site Reliability Engineer with a strong background in on-premises infrastructure, specializing in Kubernetes security and the Cloud Native ecosystem. After earning all five Kubernetes certifications, I am now expanding my expertise into modern backend development using Go and AI-driven architecture. I also hold a B.S. in Computer Science from the University of the People, which I completed while working full-time.

person.name: Desire Ntambiye
person.bio: DevOps Engineer with experience since 2020, contributing to impactful projects across multiple organizations. I have expertise with public cloud platforms including AWS, GCP, and Azure, as well as private cloud environments. I specialize in designing microservices architectures and building secure, resilient, and scalable Kubernetes-based infrastructures following DevOps best practices. My background includes two years working on retail applications and over two years delivering solutions for banking systems. I also have experience supporting machine learning projects, further strengthening my ability to work in complex, high-performance environments.

person.name: Elena Neroslavskaya
person.bio: Elena is a Cloud Solution Architect who’s passionate about AI, Kubernetes (especially CNCF), and all things open source. She loves exploring new tech, sharing ideas with the community, and building cool stuff in the cloud. She’s also a proud KubeCon speaker—always up for a good challenge and a great conversation.

person.name: Hung Tran
person.bio: Learner. Sport. Food. Joy

person.name: Hyehun Lim
person.bio: Currently, I'm a Technical Account Manager at AWS for FSI and Edu-tech. As my customers have used lots of K8S projects for their production workload, I have been studying this area for a couple of years to support them. As a Kubestronaut, I still have new things to catch up and explorer even after this. I still have a long way to go.

person.name: Muhaned Yahya
person.bio: I’m a DevOps engineer who applies cloud-native principles to automate complex operational tasks and build scalable, reliable, and resilient systems. I focus on creating Kubernetes-based developer platforms that improve consistency, security, and developer experience. I’m the creator of KubeUser (https://github.com/openkube-hub/KubeUser), an open-source project built to solve real-world Kubernetes access and user management challenges. I regularly share practical insights with the cloud-native community on LinkedIn and Medium (https://medium.com/@yahya.muhaned).

person.name: Nick Baumann
person.bio: I am a passionate Software Engineer specializing in Kubernetes-native technologies, operator development and information security.

person.name: Phil Kildea
person.bio: I have been working in Formula 1 since 2013, starting my journey as a .NET developer. Over the years, I've transitioned into a broader technical leadership role and, at the time of writing, I serve as the Head of Development Operations. I'm passionate about building scalable systems, enabling high-performance engineering teams, and driving innovation in a fast-paced environment.

person.name: Reguieg Sid Ahmed Lamine
person.bio: I'm a Senior DevOps Engineer and Systems Administrator with a strong focus on CNCF technologies, particularly Kubernetes, Docker, and Prometheus. I manage infrastructure projects, CI/CD pipelines, and cloud migrations, and I’m passionate about automation and platform reliability. I run local DevOps and Cloud Native Meetups to share knowledge and collaborate with the community.

person.name: Alex Szakaly
person.bio: Alex is always seeking to expand his knowledge and eagerly embraces new challenges. This drive led him to Kubernetes and subsequently to many other innovative CNCF projects. Whether working on deployments, tirelessly writing new Kubernetes Operators, or contributing to open-source initiatives, he brings a wealth of experience and a proactive mindset to the table. Last but not least, Alex is passionate about the vibrant community surrounding CNCF.

person.name: Catalina Cofré
person.bio: DevOps Engineer with experience in automation, CI/CD, container orchestration and Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using GitLab CI, Kubernetes, Ansible and Go/Python scripting. I aim to improve delivery workflows through clear, secure and reliable processes, supported by a curious and problem-solving mindset.

person.name: Hoekstra, Jelle
person.bio: I’m Jelle (28), based in Thailand, working as a Freelance Cloud Consultant specializing in Azure and AWS. I help organizations design secure and scalable solutions, making use of cloud-native technologies wherever possible (Kubernetes, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry and Istio mostly).
person.location: Charoen Sin, Charoen Sin District, Sakon Nakhon, Thailand

person.name: Thisura Wijesekera
person.bio: I began my career as a system engineer with a strong passion for Linux, which laid the foundation for my journey into the DevOps world. With around 5 years of experience, I’ve developed a deep interest in the CNCF landscape, particularly about kubernetes and tools like ArgoCD, Helm, Kyverno, and Keda. I’m eager to further my skills and make impactful contributions to cloud-native infrastructure and platform engineering efforts.

person.name: Álvaro "Chamo" Linares Cabré
person.bio: I'm a Cloud Engineer with over 7 years of experience in Cloud and Cloud-Native solutions.  Also I'm a instructor with over 14 years of experience in technical education.  I also have over 29 years of experience in the industry.  I've earned several international certifications, including AWS, Kubernetes, GitOps, Terraform, Linux, and Python.  😸😸😸😸

person.name: Robson Franco
person.bio: IT professional with a 15-year career, currently fueled by challenges and opportunities of Cloud Computing.

person.name: Thomas Zawisza
person.bio: I'm a DevOps Engineer with over 2 years of experience, previously working as a fullstack developer. I specialize in Azure cloud infrastructure, infrastructure-as-code with Terraform, and CI/CD automation using GitHub Actions.  Recently, I've become passionate about Kubernetes and container orchestration, working toward deeper expertise in cloud-native technologies. I'm particularly interested in building robust observability pipelines and integrating security practices throughout the development lifecycle, believing that monitoring and security should be foundational rather than afterthoughts

person.name: Chidanand Mandolkar
person.bio: I’ve spent close to a decade in technical consulting and customer success, and I am happiest where DevOps meets the cloud. I enjoy Kubernetes, CI/CD, and automation, and I like turning complex needs into clean, working solutions. I love swapping ideas, troubleshooting together, and turning tricky problems into neat wins. I stay curious, try new tech often, and bring that energy to every team I join.

person.name: Mohamed Diaby
person.bio: I am working as a DevOps and Cloud engineer. I have a strong interest for the Kubernetes ecosystem and AWS services. I strive to deepen my knowledge in distributed systems and cloud-native technologies while exploring the field of Platform Engineering.
